narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota 4Runner: brakes and air bags lead a light complaint list
narrative.summary: Owners filed just 31 complaints with NHTSA, and they spread fairly evenly. Service brakes and air bags tie for the top spot at 16% each, followed by steering and suspension (13% each) and back over prevention (10%). No fires or deaths are reported anywhere. The standout for safety is air bags: all 4 crashes and all 4 injuries in the data trace to that one area, so it is worth the closest look.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Tied for most reported: 5 reports (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Test the brake feel on a drive, since owners describe a soft pedal.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Also 5 reports (16%), but the most serious by outcome: every one of the 4 crashes and 4 injuries in this data is tied to air bags. Several owners report the bags failing to deploy.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 4 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Owners describe a shake in the wheel at highway speeds, so try a freeway stretch on the test drive.

issues.title: Suspension
issues.blurb: 4 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. A smaller, recurring complaint area on this truck.

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: The fewest reports here: 3 (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged.
